title: Common Beginner Mistakes
summary: Avoid the traps every beginner falls into

steps.title: The Big Five
steps.text: Every beginner makes these mistakes: (1) moving the same piece twice in the opening, (2) forgetting to castle, (3) bringing the queen out too early, (4) making "automatic" moves without checking, (5) playing without a plan.

steps.title: Don't Grab Every Pawn
steps.text: Not every capture is good. Sometimes taking a pawn wastes time — your opponent develops while you move the same piece again. Ask: "Is this pawn worth a tempo?"

steps.title: Watch Your Opponent's Threats
steps.text: Half of losing positions come from ignoring the opponent. When they move, always ask: "What does this move attack?" If you can't see it, you probably missed it.
